usage_sections: The USENIX Security presentation gives a summary of the discovered vulnerabilities.
An overview of all assigned CVEs is available.
Slides that summarize the root cause and impact of each vulnerability.
A 2-page summary of resulting attacks and preconditions.
Handouts that give extra background and explain the vulnerabilities in more detail.

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --version
arguments.description: Print the tool version and exit.

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

chain_examples.note: Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with younggod16/Metasploitable2-vsftpd-2.3.4-FTP-Backdoor-Exploit (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with vanhoefm/krackattacks (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Shares attack_surface+phase_hint+tag with vaibhavkhare45/Metasploitable2-Exploitation-Walkthrough-VSFTPD-2.3.4-Backdoor (score=10); pair in the chain planner.
Phase 3: same attack_surface=['web', 'ble_4_x'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 4: same attack_surface=['web', 'ble_4_x'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.
Phase 5: same attack_surface=['web', 'ble_4_x'] and phase_hint='any'; this entry chains with adjacent tools in the same family. The full set of siblings is enumerated by the catalog search index.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web, ble_4_x] in the wireless_ble_ext category. Fits when the engagement needs: FragAttacks: Fragmentation & Aggregation Attacks.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: FragAttacks: Fragmentation & Aggregation Attacks.
documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: git clone https://github.com/vanhoefm/fragattacks.git fragattacks; git clone https://github.com/vanhoefm/fragattacks-drivers58.git fragattacks-drivers58. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `options mt76_usb disable_usb_sg=1`. FragAttacks: Fragmentation & Aggregation Attacks.

name: fragattacks

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need wireless_ble_ext, use fragattacks because FragAttacks: Fragmentation & Aggregation Attacks.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `options mt76_usb disable_usb_sg=1` as documented upstream for fragattacks.
When chaining wireless_ble_ext tools, pair fragattacks with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `fragattacks --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
